messages.content:
An online retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. Which component of the enterprise platform will help the company capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services?


Values and Principles


Core Process


Experience


Data and Insights
Data and Insights.

Which describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud?


All enterprise platforms are cloud-based.


Enterprise platforms are an alternative to hosting solutions on the cloud.


Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud.


Data on the cloud can be analyzed and monitored without the need for a platform.
Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud.

Which areas of a business would most benefit from using the Workday platform?


private equity issuance and listing on a public exchange


human resources, compliance, recruiting, and finance


compliance, server machine administration, and anti-money laundering


enterprise resource planning, supply chain, and inventory management
human resources, compliance, recruiting, and finance.

Which statement describes an enterprise platform?


It is a technology that directly connects a supplier with the consumer to complete a task or achieve a goal.


It is a platform that directly connects a supplier with a consumer through a social networking interface to complete end-to-end business.


It is a platform that helps people connect and share content with colleagues and friends for social and business purposes.


It is a framework that connects people, processes, and other technologies together to deliver on a strategic business goal.
It is a framework that connects people, processes, and other technologies together to deliver on a strategic business goal.

What is an example of work performed by an Integration Platform as a Service (IPaaS)?


transferring data between multiple third-party systems


handling administration of a company’s payroll and benefits


setting up and configuring cloud-based servers


connecting buyers and sellers in a virtual marketplace
Transferring data between multiple third-party systems.

A major coffee retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. In particular, the company is looking to improve its supply chain management.

Which component of an enterprise platform focuses on this aspect of the company’s needs?


Core Processing


Data


Collaborative


Experience
Data.

A company is acquiring a smaller firm and is setting up a new IT system in order to consolidate the data and assets of the acquisition.

When evaluating enterprise platforms for the new company, what is the primary aspect to consider?


the number of platforms needed


size of the dataset to be reconciled


integration of the legacy platforms


the location/capacity of the datacenters
Integration of the legacy platforms.

What is the core function of an enterprise platform?


It hosts all businesses and other platforms.


It manages the primary functions of a business.


It connects the supplier with the consumer.


It helps employees interact with their supervisors.
It manages the primary functions of a business.

How can an organization customize and extend the functionality of a platform?


By interacting with its remote procedure calls (RPCs) to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization.


By interacting with its APIs to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization.


By requesting the platform vendor to share the source code of a product so it can be customized on-premise by local developers.


By customizing the platform's auto-configuration script according to the needs of the organization.
By interacting with its APIs to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization.

When evaluating platforms, what is the key factor in ensuring a platform addresses an organization’s needs?


mapping the platform against defined business objectives


choosing the platform with the lowest cost of implementation


using technical documentation for similar legacy applications as a reference


choosing the platform that can support the most users simultaneously
Mapping the platform against defined business objectives.

A new Accenture client is looking for a platform that will help them in hiring for critical roles and decreasing attrition. Which Accenture tool will help the client select the best type of platform for overcoming these challenges?


DevOps


myConcerto


myIndustry


SynOps
myConcerto.

In the context of automation, what is a robot?


a piece of software designed to assist a human user


a physical machine designed to help a human user to perform a specific task


a physical machine built to replicate a task that a human performs


a piece of software designed to replicate a repetitive task
A piece of software designed to replicate a repetitive task.

How does Accenture view automation?


as a universal approach that can be equally applied to all prospective clients


as a collection of shortcuts that mostly focus on basic work processes


as a spectrum that recognizes that clients have different maturity levels


as a philosophy that focuses on cost reduction above all else
As a spectrum that recognizes that clients have different maturity levels.

A regional bank implemented an automated solution to streamline their operations for receiving and processing checks. They would like to further enhance the solution to recognize signs of potential fraud and alert their security teams accordingly.

Which technology could be combined with the current solution to do this?


Sentiment Analysis


Machine Learning


Robotic Process Automation


Mechanization
Machine Learning.

Which term describes the process of training a machine to do simple, repetitive tasks, and adapt or correct its performance based on changing conditions at speed and scale?


Personal Automation


Machine Learning


Robotic Process Automation


Intelligent Automation
Robotic Process Automation.

An employee sets up an automation that transfers files in a specific folder on their PC to a remote drive for archiving, provided the files are more than six months old.

Which quality of automation is described in this example?


cumulative


rule-based


complex


intrusive
Rule-based.

What does a "swivel chair" work task involve?


validating complex information from an application


manually entering the same data into multiple systems


applying human judgment and decision making


physically engaging with several people simultaneously
Manually entering the same data into multiple systems.

Which describes the benefits of automation?


The benefits are individually small, but accumulate over time.


The benefits primarily apply to more difficult and complex processes.


The benefits are typically large and always realized in the short term.


The benefits come at the cost of speed and quality.
The benefits are individually small but accumulate over time.

A large organization is struggling to close the gaps in skill levels that exist between its employees and those from competing companies.

How can automation help the organization solve this problem?


by hiring all new employees that can handle more complex tasks


by handling the most complex work tasks through automation


by freeing up employees to train and upskill for higher-level work


by creating a greater number of automated bots than its competitors
By freeing up employees to train and upskill for higher-level work.

A Project Manager (PM) identifies several processes within a team that could be automated to reduce the manual, repetitive tasks they perform. The PM is looking for help creating an automation strategy and deciding the best approach to move forward.

Which team in their organization can the PM contact for help?


Center of Excellence


IT Support Desk


Human Resources


Automation Vendors
Center of Excellence.

What is an example of something that can be done through Personal Automation?


reading and analyzing the language in customer emails


scanning job applications to identify qualified candidates


creating a web-based calendar reminder for birthdays


using chat bots to answer customers' technical queries
Creating a web-based calendar reminder for birthdays.

What is an example of an Intelligent Automation solution that makes use of Artificial Intelligence?


recognizing and classifying images


reading data in a pre-defined table


transferring files between folders


copying text from a web browser
Recognizing and classifying images.

What is the role of a design tool in a Robotic Process Automation (RPA) solution?


to store the definitions of automated robots for re-use across an organization


to build automated robots and define the jobs they perform


to grant user permissions for interacting with automated robots


to limit the resources automated robots are permitted to consume
To build automated robots and define the jobs they perform.

What describes Accenture's approach to automation, both internally as well as for clients?


applying a one-size-fits-all automation strategy for every client


focusing primarily on technology and tools to drive automation


using automation to make businesses and people more effective


implementing advanced robotics capabilities on every automation effort
Using automation to make businesses and people more effective.

A small e-commerce company uses a series of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) solutions to manage the backup of data from individual workstations to an on-premise server. However, the company is opening another office and would like to more easily share data between locations, along with better protecting the data from loss.

Which technology could be combined with their current automated processes to do this?


Robotics


Cloud


Mechanization


Blockchain
Cloud.

What is the first step in developing an automation strategy?


selecting a vendor to build the proposed automation solution


developing a comprehensive automation training program


identifying where automation can make the most impact


determining the number of automated bots each process requires
Identifying where automation can make the most impact.

A retailer is able to track which products draw the most attention from its customers through the use of 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store. With this knowledge, the retailer can supply the proper amount of products to meet customer demand.

Which technology combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way?


internet of things


network slicing


platooning


beamforming
Internet of Things.

Why does 5G mmWave require more cells to achieve a better signal?


Higher frequencies have a shorter range.


Lower bandwidth requires consistent coverage.


Higher bandwidth requires more transmitters.


Lower frequencies are more easily blocked.
Higher frequencies have a shorter range.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data
By supporting greater numbers of connected devices.

What is a benefit of 5G mmWave technology?


It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.


It has low frequency, allowing it to transmit over longer ranges.


It provides coverage to low-density rural and suburban areas.


It easily passes through obstacles such as walls and trees.
It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.

If a person's mobile phone was designed to work with 4G technology, what will happen when a 5G network is rolled out in their area?


Their phone will automatically upgrade itself to work with the new 5G network.


Their phone will only work when they are out of range of any 5G networks.


They will lose service as the 4G network is deactivated to make room for the 5G network.


They will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years.
They will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years.

An electronics company develops a line of augmented reality headsets for use in interactive 3D media applications using 5G mmWave technology. However, customers in rural areas have been complaining about issues with an inability to connect more than their urban and suburban counterparts.

What could be contributing to the connectivity issues encountered by the rural customers?


The cell towers in the rural areas are not large enough to support mmWave.


The connection density in the rural areas is too high, causing reduced bandwidth.


The signals from the headsets are being downgraded to an earlier generation.


The rural areas do not have enough cell locations to support mmWave.
The cell towers in the rural areas are not large enough to support mmWave.

What is a distinguishing feature of 5G mmWave?


It has better reception for indoor use.


It can transfer large amounts of data quickly.


It can easily penetrate walls and barriers.


It can support an infinite number of devices.
It can transfer large amounts of data quickly.

What describes the relationship between 5G and edge computing?


5G networks will make edge computing obsolete.


5G expands the possibilities of edge computing.


Edge is a new network that competes with 5G.


Edge computing is not possible without 5G.
5G expands the possibilities of edge computing.

What does the Internet of Things (IoT) enable?


It controls network traffic in highly populated areas.


It increases the transmission range of network signals.


It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.


It translates signals between different network generations.
It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.

A frozen-food factory needs to monitor temperatures on machines, check inventory of supplies, and provide sensors on lights and doors.

What should the factory do to address these needs?


add antennas inside and outside the building


install devices that utilize edge computing


use more cell sites to better record daily activity


improve the latency over the wireless network
Install devices that utilize edge computing.

A large manufacturing company is looking to optimize worker safety on the shop floor.

What is a way the company can utilize edge computing to help achieve this?


Deploy robots that move across the shop floor and clean the work area before new workers can enter the workspace.


Use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs.


Require supervisors to verify workers are wearing the correct protective gear before they can enter the shop floor.


Provide safety training videos at workstations on the shop floor to ensure proper machine operation by workers.
Use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


a theater wants to record performances for its website


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences
An offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data.

What is Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing?


the number of custom-built cell sites we have implemented


our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies


our market share in the fields of cell networks and computing


the amount of funds we have invested in cutting-edge technology
Our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies.

An air purifier manufacturer is looking for ideas on using edge computing to enhance its products and services.

Which approach would make use of edge computing technologies?


a scheduling feature that activates a customer's air purifier at a defined time each day


a local support staff based in the customer's geographic location for more rapid responses


an automated program that can interpret customer service requests and assign agents accordingly


a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed
A device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed.

Which of the following is critical to manage the growing negative environmental impact of technology?


Identify a single stakeholder to manage the growing negative impact.

Decouple the growth of technology from its negative environmental impact.

Reduce the use of technology to lower its harmful environmental impact.
Decouple the growth of technology from its negative environmental impact.

By 2040, the information and communications technology (ICT) industry may account for ____ of global greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.


25%

30%

14%

4%

Which of the following Accenture proprietary assets helps in setting a baseline of an IT organization’s carbon footprint?


myNav Green Advisor

myNav Sustainable IT Advisor

myDiagnostic Project Green Quotient
myNav Sustainable IT Advisor.

Identify the two key steps for organizations to achieve a green software transformation.


Enabling superior disaster recovery and enhancing digital security

Setting the right environmental goals and changing the organizational mindset

Using state-of-the-art technology and tools and implementing better documentation processes
Setting the right environmental goals and changing the organizational mindset.

Which of the following is not a critical success factor for an organization in achieving its green software ambitions?


Effective organizational change

Leadership commitment

Regulatory support

The right talent and skills
Regulatory support.

Which of the following is a way to make software more green?


Adjusting the software development life cycle to be leaner and cleaner

Making efficient use of human resources

Making software more secure

Using software for greener use such as tracking deforestation
Adjusting the software development life cycle to be leaner and cleaner.

Identify the three important areas that organizations can consider to successfully transform to a green software mindset.


Green IT infrastructure, green cloud, and green software

Green software, green capabilities, and green governance

Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools

Green processes, green cloud, and green operating models
Green IT infrastructure, green operating models, and green technology and tools.

Which of the following is not true about the Green Software Foundation?


It promotes awareness about green software to build professional expertise.

It provides industry standards, metrics, guidelines, and tools to measure and reduce green software’s carbon footprint.

It brings businesses, academia, start-ups, and non-profit organizations together to advance the field of green software.

It is a partnership between Accenture, GitHub, Amazon, ThoughtWorks, and the Oracle Foundation.

It is a partnership between Accenture, GitHub, Amazon, ThoughtWorks, and the Oracle Foundation. (This statement is not true, as the Green Software Foundation has not been formed by these organizations.)

Which of the following is not a characteristic of companies that follow a single, combined path for transformation?


Directing R&D investment and initiatives toward sustainability and technology simultaneously

Taking action to minimize the negative environmental impact of technology

Focusing on business ideas that leverage technology to deliver sustainability impact

Upgrading technology for next-level digital transformation without prioritizing sustainability
Upgrading technology for next-level digital transformation without prioritizing sustainability.
